event: Madonna with Angel and Infant Saint John Adoring the Christ Child
symbols: apple or fruit in Mary's hands — the fruit of redemption, reversing Eve's forbidden fruit
infant John in prayer — the forerunner worshipping the one he would later proclaim as Lamb of God
angel holding the Christ Child — heavenly care supporting the incarnate God
globe or orb — Christ's sovereignty over the world he has entered as an infant
description: A symmetrical composition showing the Virgin Mary standing centrally in a blue mantle over a red dress, holding a round fruit (apple). To her left, a winged angel holds the nude Christ Child, who sits on what appears to be a large white globe or orb. To her right, the infant John the Baptist sits on a rock with hands joined in prayer, gazing toward Christ. The background shows a gentle green landscape with distant mountains and trees. The style is characteristic of the Umbrian Renaissance with soft colors and balanced composition.
theological_description: This devotional composition gathers the key figures of the Incarnation narrative in a timeless symbolic arrangement. Mary, standing as the central axis, holds an apple — an allusion to the fruit of Eden (Genesis 3:6) now redeemed through her consent to bear the Savior. The Christ Child seated on a globe proclaims his cosmic sovereignty: 'He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn over all creation. For in him all things were created' (Colossians 1:15-16). The infant John the Baptist kneels in adoration, prefiguring his adult ministry of pointing to Jesus: 'He must become greater; I must become less' (John 3:30). The attending angel recalls Gabriel's original announcement to Mary (Luke 1:26-38) and the heavenly host that celebrated Christ's birth. Together these figures form a visual theology of the Incarnation — heaven (the angel), Old Covenant (John as last prophet), and redeemed humanity (Mary) all converging on the divine child who holds the world in his hands.

scripture_refs.reason: The Incarnation — Mary bearing the Son of God through the Holy Spirit
Christ as firstborn over all creation — the globe on which the child sits
John's future declaration of Christ's supremacy — prefigured in his infant adoration
The fruit of the Fall — Mary holds the apple as the new Eve who reverses the curse

_meta.title: Madonna, an Angel and Little St. John Adoring the Child
